- Millipede: arthropod (myriapod)

Now I need to ask a question to distinguish among these four. Possible questions:
- Is it an insect? (Bee and Ant are insects; Centipede and Millipede are not insects but myriapods)
- Does it have wings? (Bee has wings; Ant, Centipede, Millipede don't - though some ants have wings in certain stages, generally worker ants don't)
- Does it have six legs? (Bee and Ant have six legs; Centipedes have one pair per segment, Millipedes have two pairs per segment)
- Is it typically found in gardens? (All could be, but maybe some more likely)
- Is it known for producing honey? (Only Bee)
- Does it have a stinger? (Bee has a stinger; some ants can sting but not all; centipedes and millipedes don't have stingers)
- Does it have more than 100 legs? (Millipede can have many legs, though not always 1000; Centipede has fewer)
- Is it longer than 1 inch? (Centipedes and millipedes can be longer than bees and ants typically)

Good strategy: Try to eliminate as many as possible. The question "Is it an insect?" would separate Bee and Ant (insects) from Centipede and Millipede (not insects). This would create two groups of two. That's a good binary split.

Let me ask: "Is it an insect?"
